relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: In the third month, the freed people arrive at Sinai.
Israel camps before the mountain.
Moses ascends, and YHWH speaks to house of Jacob.
Before command comes memory: I carried you on eagle wings to myself.
Listening and covenant-keeping form a treasured vocation in an earth already belonging to YHWH.
The liberated people are called kingdom of priests and holy nation.
Moses places the words before the elders.
The people answer together: all YHWH spoke, we will do.
The thick cloud lets the people hear Divine speech and trust the mediator.
Sanctification begins with washed garments.
The third day becomes the day of descent.
The mountain is bounded because holiness is overwhelming.
The horn will mark when approach becomes possible.
Moses sanctifies the people.
The people prepare for the third day.
Thunder, flashes, cloud, and horn make the camp tremble.
Moses brings the people out to meet Divine.
Sinai smokes with fire and trembles.
The horn grows stronger as Moses speaks and Divine answers.
YHWH descends and calls Moses upward.
The people are warned not to break through sight into danger.
Those who draw near must be sanctified.
Moses remembers the boundary already set.
Moses and Aaron may come, but the people and priests must not break through.
Moses returns to speak the warning.

contemplative_note: Exodus 19 is covenant arrival: carried on eagle wings, called into priestly vocation, standing before fire-cloud holiness that liberates before it commands.
